creator: rhurlin@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Yuri Victorovich from comment #0)
Hi Yuri,

As far as I understand, the both ports do not install the same. There is only a conflict with older installations.

#pkg info -l py37-sip-5.4.0,1 | grep qt
	/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sipbuild/module/source/12.7/qtlib.c
	/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/sipbuild/module/source/12.8/qtlib.c

#pkg info -l py37-qt5-sip-12.8.0 | grep qt
py37-qt5-sip-12.8.0:
	/usr/local/share/licenses/py37-qt5-sip-12.8.0/GPLv2
	/usr/local/share/licenses/py37-qt5-sip-12.8.0/GPLv3
	/usr/local/share/licenses/py37-qt5-sip-12.8.0/LICENSE
	/usr/local/share/licenses/py37-qt5-sip-12.8.0/SIP
	/usr/local/share/licenses/py37-qt5-sip-12.8.0/catalog.mk


I assume you try to install via portmaster or something similar, not via pkg?

Could you please try in this order

portmaster -o devel/py-sip py37-sip-4.19.24,1
portmaster devel/py-qt5-sip
